name: Bulochki’s Ezuminami - Rolls with Raisins
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the dry yeast in warm water.
2. Heat the 1/2 cup light cream to just below boiling, then pour over the mixture of sugar, salt, and butter in a mixing bowl. Stir until the butter melts.
3. Cool the mixture to lukewarm. Stir in the dissolved yeast, lemon zest, eggs, and enough flour to make a soft dough. Cover and chill the dough in the refrigerator for 3-4 hours (or overnight).
4. Filling:
- Beat cream cheese until smooth. Stir in 1/3 cup light cream. Mix until all lumps are gone, then mix in raisins.
5. Roll out the dough on a floured surface to 1/8 inch thickness. Cut into squares. Place a heaping tablespoon of the filling in the center of each square.
6. Bring the edges together in the center and pinch to seal. (The rolls may be baked now or chilled overnight and baked in the morning.)
7. Grease a large baking sheet. Place the rolls, pinched side down, on it and brush them with the melted butter. Cover and let rise until double in bulk.
8. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
9. Bake the rolls for 12 minutes or until brown. Best served warm.
